title: Pearl Jam’s Eddie Vedder, other luminaries spotted at Boston Springsteen show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Eddie Vedder attended Bruce Springsteen's concert at TD Garden in Boston, enjoyed the show from the pit.
J. Geils Band's Peter Wolf attended the concert, recently received an honorary doctorate from MassArt.
MIRA director Relle Maroni Minter met Springsteen; MIRA featured as concert's nonprofit, received donations.

title: 21-year-old arrested for Dorchester shooting that injured one
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Chivaugn Nettles, 21, was arrested for a Dorchester shooting that injured one person on May 13.
Nettles faces felony charges, including assault and battery by discharge of a firearm and unlawful possession of a firearm.
Nettles pleaded not guilty and is held without bail pending a dangerousness hearing in Boston Municipal Court.

title: This New England metro has an ‘emerging’ new luxury real estate market
author: Robyn A. Friedman
bullets: Portland-South Portland, Maine, is identified by Realtor.com as a top emerging luxury real estate market in New England.
Luxury market in Portland starts at $1,649,950 with 21.4% of listings priced at $1 million or more.
Factors driving Portland's luxury market include remote work, demand from outside Maine, and high housing costs in Boston.

title: Boston EMS superintendent-in-chief dies after 40 years with the department
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Gill, superintendent-in-chief of Boston EMS, died Saturday after 40 years of service with the department.
Gill started with Boston EMS in 1986, earning promotions to Paramedic, Deputy Superintendent, and Superintendent-in-Chief by 2020.
Gill contributed to founding the Boston EMS Honor Guard and the Boston EMS Relief Association, representing union members.

title: 5 Bruins prospects who could push for a spot in Boston’s lineup next season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Michael DiPietro, AHL’s top goalie, may compete for a spot as Boston's backup netminder.
Frederic Brunet could be an option on Boston's D-line as a fleet-footed puck mover.
Matt Poitras aims to add speed and scoring in a potential fourth-line role for the Bruins.

title: Lawrence man charged with murder after woman dies from serious head injury
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Tyler Halley, 21, charged with murder after woman found dead with head injury in Lawrence.
Lawrence police and others investigating death following 911 call to 240 Canal St. apartment.
Victim died at Merrimack Health Lawrence Hospital; identity not yet released by authorities.

title: ‘Purest values of service’: Communities pay tribute after veteran Boston firefighter, Marine veteran killed battling Dorchester blaze
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Veteran Boston firefighter and Marine, Robert 'Bobby' Kilduff Jr., died battling a fire at a Dorchester residence Saturday night.
Kilduff, stationed with Rescue Company 2, was a third-generation firefighter and served the Boston Fire Department for 24 years.
The Tunnel to Towers Foundation will pay off Kilduff's home mortgage and cover his children's educational expenses.

title: Jaylen Brown challenges Stephen A. Smith to debate at site like Harvard or MIT
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown challenges Stephen A. Smith to a live debate at Harvard or MIT about traditional media versus athletes.
Brown expressed frustration with the media industry and criticized Smith for not doing real journalism and promoting clickbait.
The feud began after Celtics' playoff exit, with escalating online exchanges between Brown and Smith in recent weeks.

title: NBA insider: Celtics, Heat are 2 teams ‘believed to intrigue’ Giannis in a blockbuster trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo is linked to potential trades with the Boston Celtics and Miami Heat, reports NBA insider Marc Stein.
The Celtics might need to trade Jaylen Brown in a multi-team deal to acquire Antetokounmpo from the Milwaukee Bucks.
Atlanta Hawks, Houston Rockets, and Portland Trail Blazers have shown interest in Brown for potential three-team trade scenarios.

title: Read what Bruce Springsteen had to say about Trump, ICE, and America in Boston Sunday
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bruce Springsteen criticized former President Trump and his administration during his Boston concert.
Springsteen highlighted issues such as federal troops in Minneapolis and immigrants in detention centers.
He called for hope, democracy, and unity, urging fans to fight for American ideals.
